events.event_summary: Apple's reported AI memory ramp validates Micron's bullish HBM/AI DRAM thesis, sending MU up ~16%.
primary_drivers: HBM/AI DRAM demand validation lifts entire memory pricing cycle
Apple AI device memory content increase reads through to mobile DRAM/NAND suppliers
Memory capex pull-forward benefits WFE/equipment vendors

beneficiaries.rationale: Pure-play NAND peer; tighter memory supply and rising AI storage demand lifts NAND ASPs and re-rates WDC alongside MU.
Recently spun-out NAND pure-play directly leveraged to the same memory pricing cycle Apple's AI ramp is tightening.
Lead WFE supplier for DRAM/HBM and 3D NAND; memory capex pull-forward from AI ramp drives order book.
Major exposure to memory equipment; benefits from MU/Samsung/Hynix HBM and DRAM capacity additions to serve Apple AI demand.
Process control tools needed for HBM stacking and advanced DRAM nodes; rides the same memory capex wave.
Ion implant exposure to memory fabs; smaller-cap leveraged play on memory capex pull-forward.

losers.rationale: Higher HBM/DRAM input costs compress AI server margins if memory pricing umbrella keeps rising into tight supply.
AI server BOM cost rises as memory tightens; gross margin pressure on PowerEdge AI configurations.

headlines: Apple Just Confirmed Micron's Biggest AI Prediction
